metadata.title: U.S. Visa Classification System — Complete Node-Link Dataset
sources: 22 CFR Part 41 — Visas: Documentation of Nonimmigrants (§41.12 classification symbols)
22 CFR Part 42 — Visas: Documentation of Immigrants (§42.11 classification symbols)
22 CFR Subchapter E — Title 22, Chapter I (ecfr.gov/current/title-22/chapter-I/subchapter-E)

nodes.name: Foreign Ambassador / Diplomat
nodes.description: Ambassador, public minister, career diplomatic or consular officer accredited by a foreign government, and immediate family members.

nodes.name: Foreign Government Official / Employee
nodes.description: Other accredited official or employee of a foreign government, and immediate family members.

nodes.name: Attendant of A-1 / A-2
nodes.description: Attendant, servant, or personal employee of A-1 or A-2 visa holder, and immediate family.

nodes.name: Business Visitor
nodes.description: Temporary visitor for business — consultations, conferences, negotiations; no U.S.-source employment.

nodes.name: Tourist / Medical Treatment
nodes.description: Temporary visitor for pleasure, tourism, or medical treatment.

nodes.name: Border Crossing Card (Mexico)
nodes.description: Laser visa/BCC issued to Mexican nationals for short border-zone visits.

nodes.name: Transit Alien
nodes.description: Foreign national in immediate and continuous transit through the United States.

nodes.name: Combined Transit / Crewmember
nodes.description: Combined visa for transit through the U.S. and service as a crewmember.

nodes.name: Transit to UN Headquarters
nodes.description: Foreign national in transit to or from the United Nations headquarters district.

nodes.name: Foreign Official in Transit
nodes.description: Foreign government official, immediate family, or attendant in transit through the United States.

nodes.name: Crewmember (Same Vessel)
nodes.description: Crewmember of a sea vessel or aircraft who will depart on the same vessel.

nodes.name: Crewmember (Different Vessel)
nodes.description: Crewmember of a fishing vessel with home port in Guam who will depart on a different vessel.

nodes.name: Treaty Trader
nodes.description: National of a treaty country engaged in substantial trade principally between the U.S. and the treaty country, plus spouse and children.

nodes.name: Treaty Investor
nodes.description: National of a treaty country who has invested or is actively investing a substantial amount of capital in a U.S. enterprise, plus spouse and children.

nodes.name: CNMI Long-Term Investor
nodes.description: Long-term foreign investor in the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

nodes.name: Australian Specialty Occupation
nodes.description: Australian national in a specialty occupation, analogous to H-1B, plus spouse and children.

nodes.name: Academic Student
nodes.description: Full-time student at an accredited academic institution or language training program (SEVP-certified).

nodes.name: Dependent of F-1
nodes.description: Spouse or minor child of an F-1 student. May not accept employment.

nodes.name: Border Commuter Academic Student
nodes.description: Canadian or Mexican national commuting across the border to attend a U.S. academic institution part-time.

nodes.name: Principal Representative to Int'l Org
nodes.description: Principal resident representative of a recognized foreign government to an international organization, staff, and immediate family.

nodes.name: Other Int'l Org Representative
nodes.description: Other accredited representative of a recognized foreign government to an international organization, and immediate family.

nodes.name: Representative of Non-Recognized Government
nodes.description: Representative of a non-recognized or non-member government to an international organization, and immediate family.

nodes.name: International Organization Officer / Employee
nodes.description: Officer or employee of an international organization, and immediate family.

nodes.name: Attendant of G-1 through G-4
nodes.description: Attendant, servant, or personal employee of G-1 through G-4 visa holders, and immediate family.

nodes.name: Specialty Occupation Worker
nodes.description: Worker in a specialty occupation requiring at least a bachelor's degree or equivalent. Subject to annual cap (65,000 + 20,000 advanced degree).

nodes.name: FTA Professional (Chile / Singapore)
nodes.description: National of Chile or Singapore in a specialty occupation under bilateral free trade agreements.

nodes.name: Temporary Agricultural Worker
nodes.description: Temporary or seasonal agricultural worker. Employer must obtain DOL temporary labor certification.

nodes.name: Temporary Non-Agricultural Worker
nodes.description: Temporary worker in non-agricultural services; one-time, seasonal, peak-load, or intermittent need. Annual cap of 66,000.

nodes.name: Trainee / Special Education Visitor
nodes.description: Individual coming to the U.S. to receive training not available in their home country, or to participate in a special education exchange visitor program.

nodes.name: Dependent of H Worker
nodes.description: Spouse or unmarried child (under 21) of an H-1B, H-1B1, H-2A, H-2B, or H-3 principal worker.

nodes.name: Foreign Media Representative
nodes.description: Representative of foreign press, radio, film, or other information media, and spouse and children.

nodes.name: Exchange Visitor
nodes.description: Participant in a designated Exchange Visitor Program (au pairs, scholars, interns, physicians, professors, research scholars, etc.). SEVIS-tracked.

nodes.name: Dependent of J-1
nodes.description: Spouse or minor child of a J-1 exchange visitor. May apply for work authorization.

nodes.name: Fiance(e) of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Fiancé(e) of a U.S. citizen who intends to marry within 90 days of admission. Dual-intent visa bridging to immigrant status.

nodes.name: Child of K-1
nodes.description: Minor child of K-1 fiancé(e).

nodes.name: Spouse of U.S. Citizen (LIFE Act)
nodes.description: Spouse of U.S. citizen with a pending I-130 petition, admitted to await immigrant visa processing in the U.S.

nodes.name: Child of K-3
nodes.description: Minor child of K-3 spouse.

nodes.name: Intracompany Transferee — Manager / Executive
nodes.description: Employee transferred to a U.S. affiliate/subsidiary/parent in a managerial or executive capacity. Requires 1 year employment abroad in prior 3 years.

nodes.name: Intracompany Transferee — Specialized Knowledge
nodes.description: Employee transferred to a U.S. affiliate/subsidiary/parent in a specialized knowledge capacity.

nodes.name: Dependent of L-1
nodes.description: Spouse or minor child of L-1A or L-1B. Spouse may obtain EAD.

nodes.name: Vocational Student
nodes.description: Student at an established vocational or other recognized non-academic institution (SEVP-certified).

nodes.name: Dependent of M-1
nodes.description: Spouse or minor child of M-1 vocational student.

nodes.name: Border Commuter Vocational Student
nodes.description: Canadian or Mexican national commuting to a U.S. vocational institution.

nodes.name: Principal NATO Representative
nodes.description: Principal permanent representative of a NATO member state and official staff.

nodes.name: Other NATO Representative
nodes.description: Other representative of a NATO member state, including dependents.

nodes.name: NATO Clerical Staff
nodes.description: Official clerical staff of NATO representative.

nodes.name: NATO Official (Other Country)
nodes.description: Official of NATO other than those qualifying as NATO-1 through NATO-3.

nodes.name: NATO Expert / Specialist
nodes.description: Expert or specialist employed on behalf of NATO in a non-representative capacity.

nodes.name: NATO Civilian Employee
nodes.description: Member of a civilian component accompanying a NATO force.

nodes.name: Attendant of NATO-1 through NATO-6
nodes.description: Attendant, servant, or personal employee of NATO-1 through NATO-6 personnel.

nodes.name: Extraordinary Ability — Sciences / Business / Education / Athletics
nodes.description: Individual with extraordinary ability in sciences, education, business, or athletics demonstrated by sustained national or international acclaim.

nodes.name: Extraordinary Ability — Arts / Film / TV
nodes.description: Individual with extraordinary ability in the arts or extraordinary achievement in the motion picture or television industry.

nodes.name: Accompanying Artist / Athlete
nodes.description: Individual accompanying and assisting an O-1 artist or athlete; essential to the performance.

nodes.name: Dependent of O-1 / O-2
nodes.description: Spouse or child of O-1 or O-2 visa holder.

nodes.name: Internationally Recognized Athlete
nodes.description: Individual or team athlete competing at an internationally recognized level of performance.

nodes.name: Internationally Recognized Entertainment Group
nodes.description: Member of an internationally recognized entertainment group.

nodes.name: Reciprocal Exchange Artist / Entertainer
nodes.description: Artist or entertainer performing under a reciprocal exchange program between U.S. and foreign organizations.

nodes.name: Culturally Unique Artist / Entertainer
nodes.description: Artist or entertainer performing under a program that is culturally unique.

nodes.name: Dependent of P-1 / P-2 / P-3
nodes.description: Spouse or child of P-1, P-2, or P-3 visa holder.

nodes.name: International Cultural Exchange Visitor
nodes.description: Participant in an international cultural exchange program providing practical training, employment, and sharing of culture.

nodes.name: Religious Worker
nodes.description: Minister or religious worker coming to work for a bona fide non-profit religious organization in the U.S.

nodes.name: Dependent of R-1
nodes.description: Spouse or child of R-1 religious worker.

nodes.name: Informant — Criminal Organization
nodes.description: Individual supplying critical reliable information to a law enforcement authority regarding a criminal organization.

nodes.name: Informant — Terrorism
nodes.description: Individual supplying critical reliable information regarding terrorism to the Department of Justice.

nodes.name: Dependent of S-5 / S-6
nodes.description: Spouse, married/unmarried child, or parent of S-5 or S-6 (if deemed necessary by the Attorney General).

nodes.name: Trafficking Victim
nodes.description: Victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons who is physically present in the U.S. and complies with reasonable law enforcement requests.

nodes.name: Spouse of T-1
nodes.description: Spouse of a T-1 trafficking victim.

nodes.name: Child of T-1
nodes.description: Child of a T-1 trafficking victim.

nodes.name: Parent of T-1 (Minor)
nodes.description: Parent of a T-1 trafficking victim who is under 21 years of age.

nodes.name: Sibling of T-1 (Minor)
nodes.description: Unmarried sibling under 18 of a T-1 trafficking victim who is under 21.

nodes.name: Adult Child of T-1
nodes.description: Adult child of a T-1 trafficking victim facing danger due to the victim's cooperation with law enforcement.

nodes.name: USMCA / NAFTA Professional
nodes.description: Canadian or Mexican professional in a designated USMCA/NAFTA occupation. No petition required for Canadians.

nodes.name: Dependent of TN
nodes.description: Spouse or minor child of TN professional. May not work.

nodes.name: Crime Victim
nodes.description: Victim of qualifying criminal activity who has suffered substantial abuse and assists in the investigation or prosecution. Annual cap of 10,000.

nodes.name: Spouse of U-1
nodes.description: Spouse of a U-1 crime victim.

nodes.name: Child of U-1
nodes.description: Child of a U-1 crime victim.

nodes.name: Parent of U-1 (Minor)
nodes.description: Parent of a U-1 crime victim who is under 21 years of age.

nodes.name: Sibling of U-1 (Minor)
nodes.description: Unmarried sibling under 18 of a U-1 crime victim who is under 21.

nodes.name: Spouse of LPR (Long-Pending)
nodes.description: Spouse of a lawful permanent resident who has an I-130 petition pending for 3+ years.

nodes.name: Child of LPR (Long-Pending)
nodes.description: Child of a lawful permanent resident who has an I-130 petition pending for 3+ years.

nodes.name: Derivative Child of V-1 / V-2
nodes.description: Child of a V-1 or V-2 visa holder.

nodes.name: CNMI Transitional Worker
nodes.description: Transitional worker in the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands during the federal immigration transition period.

nodes.name: Dependent of CW-1
nodes.description: Spouse or minor child of a CW-1 transitional worker.

nodes.name: Combined Business & Pleasure Visitor
nodes.description: Combined classification for temporary visitors for both business and pleasure purposes.

nodes.name: Registered Nurse (Shortage Area)
nodes.description: Registered nurse working in a health professional shortage area. Program expired but classification symbol remains in 22 CFR §41.12.

nodes.name: Dependent of E-3 Australian
nodes.description: Spouse or child of E-3 Australian specialty occupation worker.

nodes.name: Returning E-3
nodes.description: E-3 Australian specialty occupation worker returning to the U.S. on a previously issued, unexpired E-3 visa.

nodes.name: Parent of SK3 / SN3
nodes.description: Parent of an individual classified by DHS as SK3 (child of retired international org employee) or SN3 (child of retired NATO-6 civilian).

nodes.name: Child of N-8 or SK/SN Classified
nodes.description: Child of N-8 visa holder or individual classified by DHS as SK1, SK2, SK4, SN1, SN2, or SN4.

nodes.name: Spouse of U.S. Citizen (New Arrival)
nodes.description: Immediate relative — spouse of a U.S. citizen, consular processing.

nodes.name: Spouse of U.S. Citizen (Conditional)
nodes.description: Conditional resident spouse of a U.S. citizen (married < 2 years at admission). Must file I-751 to remove conditions.

nodes.name: Self-Petitioning Spouse of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Self-petitioning battered or abused spouse of a U.S. citizen under VAWA.

nodes.name: Widow(er) of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Widow or widower of a deceased U.S. citizen (married 2+ years, petition within 2 years of death).

nodes.name: Spouse via Fiance(e) Entry (Conditional)
nodes.description: Spouse who entered as K-1 fiancé(e), married U.S. citizen, adjusting status (conditional).

nodes.name: Spouse via Fiance(e) Entry
nodes.description: Spouse who entered as K-1 fiancé(e), married U.S. citizen, adjusting status (conditions removed).

nodes.name: Child of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Immediate relative — unmarried child under 21 of a U.S. citizen.

nodes.name: Child of U.S. Citizen (Conditional)
nodes.description: Conditional resident child accompanying conditional resident spouse.

nodes.name: Self-Petitioning Child of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Self-petitioning battered or abused child of a U.S. citizen.

nodes.name: Child of IB1 Self-Petitioner
nodes.description: Child accompanying or following to join an IB1/IB6 self-petitioning spouse.

nodes.name: Child of Widow(er)
nodes.description: Child accompanying or following to join IW1/IW6 widow(er).

nodes.name: Child of CF1 (Conditional)
nodes.description: Child of CF1 spouse (entered via K-2, conditional).

nodes.name: Child of IF1
nodes.description: Child of IF1 spouse (entered via K-2, conditions removed).

nodes.name: Amerasian Child of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Amerasian immediate relative child.

nodes.name: Orphan Adopted Abroad
nodes.description: Orphan adopted abroad by a U.S. citizen (non-Hague).

nodes.name: Orphan to be Adopted in U.S.
nodes.description: Orphan coming to the U.S. to be adopted by a U.S. citizen (non-Hague).

nodes.name: Hague Convention Adoptee (Abroad)
nodes.description: Child adopted abroad under the Hague Intercountry Adoption Convention.

nodes.name: Hague Convention Adoptee (U.S.)
nodes.description: Child coming to be adopted in the U.S. under the Hague Convention.

nodes.name: Parent of Adult U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Parent of a U.S. citizen who is at least 21 years old.

nodes.name: Self-Petitioning Parent (VAWA)
nodes.description: Battered or abused parent of U.S. citizen, self-petitioning.

nodes.name: Parent of U.S. Citizen (Virgin Islands Act)
nodes.description: Parent of U.S. citizen who acquired permanent resident status under the Virgin Islands Nonimmigrant Alien Adjustment Act.

nodes.name: F1 — Unmarried Son/Daughter of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: First family preference — unmarried sons and daughters (21+) of U.S. citizens.

nodes.name: F1 — Child of F11
nodes.description: Derivative child of an F1 preference principal.

nodes.name: F2A — Spouse of LPR
nodes.description: Second family preference (A) — spouse of a lawful permanent resident.

nodes.name: F2A — Child of LPR
nodes.description: Second family preference (A) — child under 21 of a lawful permanent resident.

nodes.name: F2A — Derivative Child of F2A Spouse/Child
nodes.description: Derivative child of F2A spouse or child principal.

nodes.name: F2B — Unmarried Son/Daughter of LPR
nodes.description: Second family preference (B) — unmarried sons and daughters (21+) of a lawful permanent resident.

nodes.name: F2B — Child of F24
nodes.description: Derivative child of an F2B principal.

nodes.name: F3 — Married Son/Daughter of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Third family preference — married sons and daughters of U.S. citizens.

nodes.name: F3 — Spouse of F31
nodes.description: Spouse of F3 principal.

nodes.name: F3 — Child of F31
nodes.description: Child of F3 principal.

nodes.name: F4 — Sibling of U.S. Citizen
nodes.description: Fourth family preference — brothers and sisters of adult U.S. citizens.

nodes.name: F4 — Spouse of F41
nodes.description: Spouse of F4 principal.

nodes.name: F4 — Child of F41
nodes.description: Child of F4 principal.

nodes.name: EB-1A — Extraordinary Ability
nodes.description: First employment preference — persons of extraordinary ability in sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics.

nodes.name: EB-1B — Outstanding Professor / Researcher
nodes.description: First employment preference — outstanding professors and researchers with 3+ years of experience.

nodes.name: EB-1C — Multinational Manager / Executive
nodes.description: First employment preference — multinational managers or executives.

nodes.name: EB-1 — Spouse
nodes.description: Spouse of EB-1 principal.

nodes.name: EB-1 — Child
nodes.description: Child of EB-1 principal.

nodes.name: EB-2 — Advanced Degree Professional / Exceptional Ability
nodes.description: Second employment preference — professionals holding advanced degrees or persons of exceptional ability. Includes NIW (National Interest Waiver).

nodes.name: EB-2 — Soviet Scientist
nodes.description: Former Soviet Union scientists admitted under special legislation.

nodes.name: EB-2 — Spouse
nodes.description: Spouse of EB-2 principal.

nodes.name: EB-2 — Child
nodes.description: Child of EB-2 principal.

nodes.name: EB-3 — Skilled Worker
nodes.description: Third employment preference — skilled workers with 2+ years training/experience.

nodes.name: EB-3 — Professional (Baccalaureate)
nodes.description: Third employment preference — professionals with baccalaureate degrees.

nodes.name: EB-3 — Other / Unskilled Worker
nodes.description: Third employment preference — other (unskilled) workers performing labor for which qualified workers are not available.

nodes.name: EB-3 — Schedule A Worker
nodes.description: Third employment preference — Schedule A pre-certified occupations (nurses, physical therapists).

nodes.name: EB-3 — Chinese Student Protection Act
nodes.description: Chinese nationals who were in the U.S. after June 4, 1989 (Tiananmen).

nodes.name: EB-3 — Spouse (Skilled/Professional)
nodes.description: Spouse of EB-3 skilled worker or professional.

nodes.name: EB-3 — Child (Skilled/Professional)
nodes.description: Child of EB-3 skilled worker or professional.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Minister of Religion
nodes.description: Fourth employment preference — special immigrant minister of religion.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Religious Worker
nodes.description: Fourth employment preference — special immigrant religious worker (non-minister).

nodes.name: EB-4 — U.S. Government Employee Abroad
nodes.description: Certain employees or former employees of the U.S. government abroad.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Panama Canal Company Employee
nodes.description: Former employee of the Panama Canal Company or Canal Zone Government.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Panama Canal Zone Gov't Employee
nodes.description: Former U.S. government employee in the Panama Canal Zone.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Panama Canal Employee (1979)
nodes.description: Former Panama Canal Company employee employed on April 1, 1979.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Foreign Medical Graduate
nodes.description: Foreign medical school graduate (adjustment only). 9 FAM symbol SJ1; DHS adjustment code SJ6.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Retired Int'l Org Employee
nodes.description: Retired employee of an international organization.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Surviving Spouse of Int'l Org Employee
nodes.description: Certain surviving spouses of deceased international organization employees.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Juvenile Court Dependent
nodes.description: Special immigrant juvenile who has been declared dependent on a juvenile court.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Armed Forces (Post-1991)
nodes.description: Alien who has served 12 years in the U.S. Armed Forces after October 1, 1991.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Armed Forces (Pre-1991)
nodes.description: Alien who served 12 years in the U.S. Armed Forces before October 1991.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Broadcasting Board Employee
nodes.description: Employee of the International Broadcasting Bureau (BBG).

nodes.name: EB-4 — Retired NATO-6 Civilian
nodes.description: Retired NATO-6 civilian employee who was in the U.S. 15+ years.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Surviving Spouse of NATO-6
nodes.description: Surviving spouse of a deceased NATO-6 civilian employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Iraqi/Afghan Interpreter
nodes.description: Special immigrant interpreter/translator who worked with U.S. Armed Forces in Iraq or Afghanistan.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Iraqi/Afghan U.S. Gov't Employee
nodes.description: Iraqi or Afghan national employed by or on behalf of the U.S. government.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of SD1
nodes.description: Spouse of special immigrant minister.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of SD1
nodes.description: Child of special immigrant minister.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of SR1
nodes.description: Spouse of special immigrant religious worker.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of SR1
nodes.description: Child of special immigrant religious worker.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of SE1
nodes.description: Spouse of U.S. government employee abroad.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of SE1
nodes.description: Child of U.S. government employee abroad.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse/Child of SF1
nodes.description: Spouse or child of Panama Canal Company employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse/Child of SG1
nodes.description: Spouse or child of former U.S. government employee in Canal Zone.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse/Child of SH1
nodes.description: Spouse or child of Panama Canal employee (1979).

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse/Child of SJ1
nodes.description: Spouse or child of SJ1 foreign medical graduate.

nodes.name: EB-4 — September 11 Beneficiary
nodes.description: Beneficiary of a petition or labor certification filed before September 11, 2001 rendered void by the terrorist attacks, or spouse/child thereof, or grandparent of a child orphaned by the attacks.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Surviving Spouse/Child of U.S. Gov't Employee Abroad
nodes.description: Surviving spouse or child of a U.S. government employee killed abroad in the line of duty.

nodes.name: GRATEFUL Act — Surviving Spouse/Child of U.S. Gov't Employee
nodes.description: Surviving spouse or child of U.S. government employee abroad, issued under the GRATEFUL Act when a visa is not immediately available under INA §203(b)(4).

nodes.name: GRATEFUL Act — U.S. Gov't Employee Abroad
nodes.description: Certain current or former employee of the U.S. government abroad, issued under the GRATEFUL Act.

nodes.name: GRATEFUL Act — Spouse of GV1
nodes.description: Spouse of GV1 U.S. government employee.

nodes.name: GRATEFUL Act — Child of GV1
nodes.description: Child of GV1 U.S. government employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of SK1
nodes.description: Spouse of retired international organization employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of SK1
nodes.description: Unmarried child of retired international organization employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of SN1
nodes.description: Spouse of retired NATO-6 civilian.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of SN1
nodes.description: Unmarried son/daughter of retired NATO-6 civilian.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of BC1
nodes.description: Spouse of BBG broadcasting employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of BC1
nodes.description: Child of BBG broadcasting employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of SI1
nodes.description: Spouse of Iraqi/Afghan interpreter.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of SI1
nodes.description: Child of Iraqi/Afghan interpreter.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of SQ1
nodes.description: Spouse of Iraqi/Afghan U.S. government employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of SQ1
nodes.description: Child of Iraqi/Afghan U.S. government employee.

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse of SM1
nodes.description: Spouse of armed forces member (post-1991).

nodes.name: EB-4 — Child of SM1
nodes.description: Child of armed forces member (post-1991).

nodes.name: EB-4 — Spouse/Child of SM4
nodes.description: Spouse or child of armed forces member (pre-1991).

nodes.name: EB-5 — Investor (Non-Targeted)
nodes.description: Fifth employment preference — employment creation investor in a non-targeted employment area. Conditional.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Investor (Targeted Area)
nodes.description: Employment creation investor in a targeted employment area (TEA). Conditional.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Investor Pilot Program (Non-Targeted)
nodes.description: Investor through regional center pilot program, non-targeted area. Conditional.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Investor Pilot Program (Targeted)
nodes.description: Investor through regional center pilot program, targeted employment area. Conditional.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Investor (Unconditional)
nodes.description: Employment creation investor, conditions removed.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Spouse (Non-Targeted)
nodes.description: Spouse of C51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Spouse (Targeted)
nodes.description: Spouse of T51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Spouse (Pilot, Non-Targeted)
nodes.description: Spouse of R51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Spouse (Pilot, Targeted)
nodes.description: Spouse of I51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Spouse (Unconditional)
nodes.description: Spouse of E51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Child (Non-Targeted)
nodes.description: Child of C51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Child (Targeted)
nodes.description: Child of T51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Child (Pilot, Non-Targeted)
nodes.description: Child of R51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Child (Pilot, Targeted)
nodes.description: Child of I51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 — Child (Unconditional)
nodes.description: Child of E51 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Non-Regional Center, Unreserved
nodes.description: Investor in non-regional center enterprise, unreserved category. Petitions filed on or after March 15, 2022.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Spouse of NU1
nodes.description: Spouse of NU1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Child of NU1
nodes.description: Child of NU1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Regional Center, Unreserved
nodes.description: Investor in regional center enterprise, unreserved category. Petitions filed on or after March 15, 2022.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Spouse of RU1
nodes.description: Spouse of RU1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Child of RU1
nodes.description: Child of RU1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Non-Regional Center, Rural Set-Aside
nodes.description: Investor in non-regional center enterprise in a rural area, set-aside category.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Spouse of NR1
nodes.description: Spouse of NR1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Child of NR1
nodes.description: Child of NR1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Non-Regional Center, High Unemployment Set-Aside
nodes.description: Investor in non-regional center enterprise in a high unemployment area, set-aside category.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Spouse of NH1
nodes.description: Spouse of NH1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Child of NH1
nodes.description: Child of NH1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Regional Center, Rural Set-Aside
nodes.description: Investor in regional center enterprise in a rural area, set-aside category.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Spouse of RR1
nodes.description: Spouse of RR1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Child of RR1
nodes.description: Child of RR1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Regional Center, High Unemployment Set-Aside
nodes.description: Investor in regional center enterprise in a high unemployment area, set-aside category.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Spouse of RH1
nodes.description: Spouse of RH1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Child of RH1
nodes.description: Child of RH1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Regional Center, Infrastructure Set-Aside
nodes.description: Investor in regional center enterprise for infrastructure projects, set-aside category.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Spouse of RI1
nodes.description: Spouse of RI1 investor.

nodes.name: EB-5 Reform — Child of RI1
nodes.description: Child of RI1 investor.

nodes.name: Diversity Visa — Principal
nodes.description: Diversity Immigrant Visa Program (lottery) principal applicant from an under-represented country. 55,000 visas annually.

nodes.name: Diversity Visa — Spouse
nodes.description: Spouse of DV1 lottery winner.

nodes.name: Diversity Visa — Child
nodes.description: Child of DV1 lottery winner.

nodes.name: Returning Resident
nodes.description: Lawful permanent resident returning to the U.S. after a temporary absence abroad exceeding 1 year or validity of re-entry permit.

nodes.name: Refugee
nodes.description: Person outside their country of nationality unable or unwilling to return due to persecution. Admitted by presidential determination.

nodes.name: Refugee — Spouse
nodes.description: Spouse of RE6 refugee following to join.

nodes.name: Refugee — Child
nodes.description: Child of RE6 refugee following to join.

nodes.name: Cuban Refugee
nodes.description: Cuban national admitted or paroled after January 1, 1959, adjusting to LPR status.

nodes.name: Indochinese Refugee
nodes.description: Indochinese refugee admitted under early parole programs.

nodes.name: Asylee — Principal
nodes.description: Person physically present in the U.S. granted asylum due to persecution. May adjust to LPR after 1 year.

nodes.name: Asylee — Spouse
nodes.description: Spouse of AS6 asylee.

nodes.name: Asylee — Child
nodes.description: Child of AS6 asylee.

nodes.name: Iraqi Asylee
nodes.description: Iraqi asylee adjusting to LPR.

nodes.name: Syrian Asylee
nodes.description: Syrian asylee adjusting to LPR.

nodes.name: Trafficking Victim Adjustment (T-1)
nodes.description: Adjustment of T-1 trafficking victim to LPR after 3 years or completion of investigation.

nodes.name: Crime Victim Adjustment (U-1)
nodes.description: Adjustment of U-1 crime victim to LPR after 3 years continuous presence.

nodes.description: Nicaraguan and Central American Relief Act — Nicaraguan/Cuban principal adjusting status.

nodes.description: Spouse of NC6 principal.

nodes.name: NACARA §202 — Child
nodes.description: Child of NC6 principal.

nodes.description: Unmarried son or daughter of NC6 principal.

nodes.description: Haitian Refugee Immigration Fairness Act — asylum applicant adjusting.

nodes.description: Haitian parolee adjusting under HRIFA.

nodes.description: Haitian child without parents adjusting under HRIFA.

nodes.description: Haitian child orphaned in the United States.

nodes.description: Haitian child abandoned by parents.

nodes.name: Cancellation of Removal — INA §244
nodes.description: Cancellation of removal (formerly suspension of deportation), subject to 4,000 annual limit.

nodes.name: Cancellation of Removal — VAWA
nodes.description: Cancellation for battered spouses or children, subject to 4,000 annual limit.

nodes.name: Cancellation of Removal — NACARA §203
nodes.description: Cancellation for Salvadoran, Guatemalan, and former Soviet bloc nationals.

nodes.name: IRCA Legalization — Pre-1982 Entry
nodes.description: IRCA legalization for aliens who entered without inspection before January 1, 1982.

nodes.name: IRCA Legalization — Visa Overstay
nodes.description: IRCA legalization for aliens who entered as nonimmigrants and overstayed before January 1, 1982.

nodes.name: IRCA Legalization — EVD Group
nodes.description: IRCA legalization for blanket enforced voluntary departure group members.

nodes.name: IRCA SAW — Group I
nodes.description: Seasonal Agricultural Worker who worked 90+ days in each year ending May 1984, 1985, and 1986.

nodes.name: IRCA SAW — Group II
nodes.description: Seasonal Agricultural Worker who worked 90+ days in year ending May 1986.

nodes.description: Spouse of legalized alien under IRCA.

nodes.description: Child of legalized alien under IRCA.

nodes.description: Amerasian born in Vietnam between 1/1/1962 and 1/1/1976 (child of U.S. military).

nodes.description: Spouse or child of AM1 Amerasian.

nodes.description: Mother, guardian, or next of kin of AM1 Amerasian.

nodes.description: Spouse of AA1.

nodes.description: Child of AA1.

nodes.description: Child of DT1.

nodes.description: Child of HK1.

nodes.name: American Indian Born in Canada
nodes.description: Person of at least 50% American Indian blood born in Canada; free passage right.

nodes.description: Former U.S. citizen who lost citizenship through service in foreign armed forces.

nodes.description: Spouse or child of NP8 investor.

nodes.description: Child born abroad to alien residents on temporary visit outside the U.S.

nodes.description: Alien who entered before July 1, 1924, adjusting via registry provision.

nodes.description: Alien who entered between July 1, 1924 and June 28, 1940, adjusting via registry.

nodes.description: Alien who entered between June 29, 1940 and January 1, 1972, adjusting via registry.

nodes.name: Private Bill
nodes.description: Alien granted LPR status through a private bill enacted by Congress.

nodes.description: Crewman who entered on or before June 30, 1964, who is an immediate relative of a U.S. citizen.

nodes.description: Crewman who entered on or before June 30, 1964, adjusting as preference or non-preference immigrant.
